The '''Thandon Cluster''',<ref name="AIR" /> also known as the '''Thandon Nebula''',<ref name="TEA" /> was a region with the characteristics of a [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]]<ref name="AIR" /> and a [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] that was situated in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]]<ref name="TEA" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> To its south ran the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Authority Guardian Corridor]], while the [[Shaltin Tunnels/Legends|Shaltin Tunnels]] [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route passed through the Thandon Cluster's western section. To the north of the area, a third hyperlane passed through its fringes, terminating at<ref name="TEA" /> the Thandon Cluster's [[Dostra system]].<ref name="AIR" />

The Dostra [[Star system/Legends|system]] contained the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Brosi]], which was the only inhabited world in the Thandon Cluster, being [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to a population of [[near-Human]]s known as the [[Brosin]]s.<ref name="AIR" /> The Thandon Cluster eventually came under the control of the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]], which struggled with developing<ref name="TEA" /> the remote area.<ref name="AIR" /> By [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Corporate Sector Authority had established [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations on Brosi, with the planet's population by that time also including [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and numbering nineteen million.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Thandon Cluster was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the Thandon Cluster planet Brosi was illustrated by [[Mike Vilardi]].<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently introduced the alternate name "Thandon Nebula" and placed the region in grid square S-3.<ref name="TEA" />
